description: In your role as the site’s backup administrator you have just finished preparing the data that lives in /home/user/important_data. The next (and only) thing you have to do is archive it properly and lock it down so nobody except you can read it. Requirements that must be satisfied after you finish (the automated tests will look for them exactly): 1. Create a single gz-compressed tar archive called /home/user/secure_backups/important_data_20231010.tar.gz that contains the complete content of the directory /home/user/important_data (not the directory’s parent). 2. Immediately after the archive is created, change its permission bits so that • owner = read + write • group = none • others = none (octal mode 600). 3. Append one (1) new line to the log file /home/user/secure_backups/backup.log in exactly the following **space-separated** four-column format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS FILENAME SIZE_IN_BYTES PERMISSIONS • FILENAME must be literally: important_data_20231010.tar.gz • SIZE_IN_BYTES must be the decimal byte size of the new archive (use any standard tool; the test only checks that this column is a non--negative integer). • PERMISSIONS must be the string 600. • Terminate the line with a single newline character “\n”. • Do not add extra spaces or other columns. Example pattern (your timestamp and size will differ): 2023-10-10 14:05:42 important_data_20231010.tar.gz 2048 600 4. Accomplish all of the above with ONE single terminal command line (you may concatenate sub-commands with “&&” or “;”, but it must be issued as a single line). Hints you may consider (but are not limited to): tar, chmod, stat, date/printf redirection. No root access is available, so stay within your normal user privileges. After you run your single command, you’re done. The test suite will: • confirm that /home/user/secure_backups/important_data_20231010.tar.gz exists • verify its permission bits are exactly 600 • make sure the archive actually contains every file currently present in /home/user/important_data • parse the last line of /home/user/secure_backups/backup.log and check that the four fields match the required pattern (filename & permissions exact, size is integer, timestamp is valid). You do not need to display anything besides what your command naturally outputs.
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied to the agent:

1. Directory /home/user/important_data
   ├── file1.txt   (content: "Alpha\n")
   └── file2.txt   (content: "Beta\n")

2. Directory /home/user/secure_backups already exists and is writable by user.
   • If /home/user/secure_backups/backup.log is present it may contain zero or more previous lines using the same four-column format.  
   • The automated tests only evaluate the last line after the agent finishes.

Post-task state that the tests will assert:

A. /home/user/secure_backups/important_data_20231010.tar.gz  
   • file exists  
   • owner permissions = rw, group = none, others = none (mode 600)  
   • is a valid gzipped tar containing exactly the two files file1.txt and file2.txt at path entries “file1.txt” and “file2.txt” (no leading “/home/user/important_data” in the archive paths).

B. /home/user/secure_backups/backup.log  
   • is a regular text file, ASCII/UTF-8  
   • its last line matches the regular expression:  
     ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\ [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\ important_data_20231010\.tar\.gz\ [0-9]+\ 600\n$  
   • (the size field must equal the actual byte size of the archive).

Nothing else is required; no additional files should be created.
name: task_000000_edfab650
